mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic services in a Kubernetes environment, leveraging the operator pattern for simplified management. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the ECK Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install ECK from the official source.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace elastic-system

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the ECK operator.
> Installs the ECK operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Deploy an Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

```

> Creates a simple Elasticsearch cluster with 3 nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

kubectl describe elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> Lists the Elasticsearch resources in the 'elastic-system' namespace.
> Describes the Elasticsearch resource for detailed status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Elasticsearch clusters are not deleted first.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
This step involves backing up existing resources, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> Backs up the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verifies the status of the Elasticsearch resources after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch cluster may not have enough resources allocated or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the Elasticsearch CR and adjust them as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Elasticsearch'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch service may not be exposed correctly or the pod may not be running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n elastic-system' to check pod status and 'kubectl describe service my-elasticsearch' to verify service configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Elasticsearch cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for Elasticsearch may not be applied correctly or the operator may not be running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the ECK operator is running with 'kubectl get pods -n elastic-system' and check the CRD with 'kubectl get crd'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to create resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account used by the ECK operator may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the role bindings for the ECK operator and ensure it has the required permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x, improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.12 or 2.13, ensure all Elasticsearch clusters are compatible with version 8.x or 9.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster listed with a status of 'green' when running 'kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
